title: CHAR Tech Receives $CAD 10M Commitment From BMI Towards 50,000 tonne per Year Espanola Biocarbon Project
content: CHAR Technologies received a CAD 10 million commitment from The BMI Group to advance engineering and design for its Espanola biocarbon project at Bioveld North in Ontario, targeting up to 50,000 tonnes of annual biocarbon output, five times the capacity of the partnersâ€™ Thorold facility. The study, slated to finish in Q1 2026, will finalize scope, feedstock supply, and integration of CHAR Techâ€™s highâ€‘temperature pyrolysis within existing site infrastructure. The proposed facility would convert locally sourced wood waste and forestry residuals into lowâ€‘carbon biocarbon and synthetic gas, with optional onâ€‘site use or later upgrading to renewable natural gas. Leaders from both companies said the project leverages Northern Ontarioâ€™s forestry residuals and existing industrial assets to move rapidly from development to construction and return the former pulp and paper mill to productive use focused on innovation, fullâ€‘fibre utilization, and cleanâ€‘energy production.
author: Analysts Account
